what_it_does: This file is a descriptive terrain-entry ledger record for a self-hosted GitHub Actions runner install; it documents what the runner is, how it is launched via systemd, what it connects to, and that it stores registration credentials.
observations: The entry describes a 1.8G install at /home/tim/actions-runner.
Two runner binary versions are kept side by side (bin, bin.2.321.0, bin.2.334.0) plus matching externals dirs.
It is bound to the external GitHub repo Concept-Vi/ollama-pipeliner.
A systemd unit github-runner.service runs run.sh with WorkingDirectory set to the runner directory.
The unit passes through PATH including /home/tim/.local/bin and HOME=/home/tim.
The PATH passthrough is explicitly intended so CI jobs can invoke claude, gh, python, etc.
The runner tree holds registration credentials (.credentials/.runner).
It is tagged control, marked secrets: true, and listed as part of [[company]] / [[company-systemd]].
Status is unconfirmed and there is no move_intent.
Coverage shows all 2 files in the tree were read.
standouts: A self-hosted runner that stores repo registration credentials locally.
PATH passthrough wires the runner to the user's local tools including claude and gh.
It acts as a bridge between external GitHub CI and the local workstation environment.
conventions: Follows the terrain-entry frontmatter schema with type, name, register, relation, kind, status, tags, and wikilinked part-of relations.
Uses a descriptive register rather than normative.
Cites verbatim evidence from the systemd unit file.
Marks secrets: true for credential-bearing infrastructure.
concerns: The runner passes the user's full PATH and HOME into CI execution, giving externally triggered workflows access to local credentials and tools.
Registration credentials live inside the runner tree on disk.
Restart=always means the runner will keep respawning.
CI jobs are configured to invoke claude and gh, which may operate with the user's personal credentials.
The entry status is unconfirmed, so current activity is not verified.
notes: This entry maps a self-hosted GitHub Actions runner that lets external GitHub workflows run on the workstation with access to local PATH, HOME, and stored credentials.
questions: What workflows/jobs are currently configured to run on this runner?
Are the stored .credentials/.runner tokens still valid and how are they rotated?
Is the PATH/HOME passthrough an intentional security posture or convenience?
Why are two runner binary versions kept side by side?
Is the repo binding Concept-Vi/ollama-pipeliner still active?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a descriptive register entry and it does describe the runner, its launch wiring, and its external binding.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to record that an external SaaS control surface (GitHub Actions) can execute code locally through a persistent agent, and to flag that this agent carries secrets and reaches local user tooling.
novelty: A self-hosted runner on a workstation configured to invoke the claude CLI inside CI jobs, with side-by-side runner upgrade directories and explicit PATH passthrough to the company CLI location.
contribution: It documents a boundary-crossing external engine, its systemd launch path, its stored credentials, and its integration with local tooling, which a fused system would keep for control-plane inventory and trust-boundary analysis.
summary_for_embedding: A terrain-entry ledger file describing a 1.8G self-hosted GitHub Actions runner at /home/tim/actions-runner, launched by github-runner.service, bound to the GitHub repo Concept-Vi/ollama-pipeliner, with PATH passthrough to local tools and stored registration credentials.
intention_for_embedding: To record the presence, launch wiring, external CI binding, and secrets exposure of a self-hosted runner that executes GitHub workflows on this workstation.
